name: Panacea
description: Panacea, in Greek mythology, is the goddess of universal remedy. She is the daughter of Asclepius, the god of medicine, and Epione, goddess of the soothing of pain. Her name literally translates to 'all-healing' or 'cure-all.' Panacea embodies the concept of a universal cure for diseases and ailments. She is often depicted with a mortar and pestle, or a vial containing healing substances. While her father Asclepius focused on the art of healing and surgery, Panacea represented the ideal of a single, all-encompassing remedy. She was revered by those seeking cures and relief from suffering. Unlike her sister Hygieia, who represented preventative health and hygiene, Panacea offered a more reactive approach, focused on curing existing illnesses. Her influence extended to apothecaries and healers who sought to create elixirs and potions that could address a wide range of health problems. While no single cure-all has ever been discovered, the ideal of Panacea continues to inspire medical research and the pursuit of comprehensive treatments. Her legacy reminds us of the enduring human desire to overcome illness and achieve complete well-being. She is a potent symbol of hope and the unwavering belief in the possibility of healing.
subtitle: Goddess of Universal Remedy

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Panacea
keyMyths.description: Panacea was born to Asclepius and Epione, inheriting her father's medical skills and her mother's soothing touch. From birth, she was destined to be a healer.

keyMyths.title: Panacea's Universal Cure
keyMyths.description: Panacea was believed to possess a remedy that could cure all diseases, making her a sought-after figure. However, this powerful cure remained elusive.

keyMyths.title: Panacea and the Healers
keyMyths.description: Apothecaries and healers often invoked Panacea in their practices, hoping to create remedies inspired by her universal cure. She represented the ideal of complete healing.

sources.author: Pseudo-Apollodorus
sources.description: A comprehensive source on Greek myths and legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive source on Greek myths and legends.

sources.author: Hyginus
sources.description: A collection of mythological stories, often providing alternate versions of well-known myths.
